metadata.title: Zekelman 2025 Standard 3 Teacher Review Tracking
metadata.review_scope: Review-only preparation for selected question-relevant Standard 3 strands before any runtime use or question generation.
notes: This file does not change runtime behavior, Streamlit/UI behavior, reviewed-bank truth, or production data.
Teacher-review guidance from Yossi Bassman is recorded here for selected items, but it remains documentation-only and not runtime-ready or question-ready.
Each review item links back to an existing Standard 3 record and one or more existing draft skill IDs.

supplemental_source_sections: 2014 intro: Pages 3-7 / Nouns
2014 appendix: Pages 22-28 and 36 / Vocabulary Appendix Standards 3.1-3.2; Sample List of Nouns Per Grade
2016 sample assessments: Pages 73-76 / 3.1 Vocabulary: שמות עצם (nouns)
review_items.reviewer_notes: Teacher review supports this as a foundational planning lane for taught noun recognition, translation, and controlled multiple-meaning work. Keep it limited to approved vocabulary lists and source-verified wording. This review does not authorize runtime activation or student-facing question generation.
unresolved_questions: Does row C on page 30 clearly mean grouping nouns by shared taught noun family, and is the extracted wording accurate enough for later reviewer use?
Should contextual multiple-meaning work remain limited to previously taught noun sets such as ראש and רוח before any broader diagnostic use?
Do the appendix noun-grade tables align cleanly enough with the 2025 cumulative counts to support later teacher-approved scope planning?
review_items.recommended_next_action: Document 3.01 as a foundational diagnostic planning lane only, verify the canonical/source wording directly in the raw PDF, and keep any later planning limited to approved vocabulary lists.

supplemental_source_sections: 2014 intro: Pages 35-40 / Verbs; Roots - שרשים; Present and Future Tense Verbs
2014 appendix: Pages 22-27 and 37 / Vocabulary Appendix Standards 3.1-3.2; Sample שורשים List
2016 sample assessments: Pages 79-87 / 3.2 Vocabulary: שרשים (verbs)
review_items.reviewer_notes: Teacher review supports simple shoresh identification as a foundational planning lane. Weak-letter roots, altered-root recognition, and more advanced contextual shoresh interpretation should be split out and deferred to later levels or later review. This review does not authorize runtime activation or student-facing question generation.
unresolved_questions: Which parts of 3.02 should be treated as foundational at first review: simple shoresh identification only, or also weak-letter and altered-root recognition?
Should contextual root-translation be reviewed as part of the same skill decision, or deferred until later verb-feature review is complete?
Do the appendix root lists and 2025 cumulative counts align well enough to support a later teacher-approved level map?
review_items.recommended_next_action: Split the draft 3.02 planning lane between foundational simple shoresh identification and deferred advanced root work, then review the level mapping before any diagnostic blueprint use.

supplemental_source_sections: 2014 intro: Pages 3-11 and 48 / Nouns; Adjectives; construct-chain note in ניקוד section
2014 appendix: Page 38 / Appendix for Parts of Speech, 3.4 B and C
2016 sample assessments: Pages 89-100 / 3.4 Nouns and Adjectives
unresolved_questions: Should noun/adjective feature review and סמיכות review remain bundled for this teacher packet, or should later phases split them into separate approval decisions?
Is standalone סמיכות identification suitable for diagnostic preparation, or should it remain tied to fuller phrase-translation context only?
How much irregular-form knowledge from 3.04 should be treated as foundational before any later question-generation planning?
review_items.recommended_next_action: Teacher-review the 3.04 wording with special attention to סמיכות, adjective agreement, and irregular noun-form expectations, then decide whether this strand should stay context-heavy in later phases.

supplemental_source_sections: 2014 intro: Pages 23-30 / Pronouns; Subjective and Objective Pronouns; Possessive Nouns and Pronouns
2014 appendix: Pages 39-44 / Pronoun appendices for Standards 3.5A-H
2016 sample assessments: Pages 101-121 / 3.5 Pronouns
review_items.reviewer_notes: Teacher review supports the diagnostic direction, but 3.05 must be split into at least two separate skill lanes: pronoun referent tracking and pronominal suffix decoding. Do not bundle these as one broad skill. This review does not authorize runtime activation or student-facing question generation.
unresolved_questions: Should referent tracking and pronominal-suffix decoding be treated as separate foundational approvals, even though they share the same 3.05 strand?
At what point is it fair to expect cross-pasuk referent tracking rather than local referent identification only?
Should the use of איתן and תהימון as pronoun clues stay with pronoun review, or be deferred until the verb-feature review is settled?
review_items.recommended_next_action: Revise the 3.05 planning language into separate pronoun-referent and pronominal-suffix lanes, then keep both lanes documentation-only until the wording split is finalized.

review_items.standard_title: Prepositions, Conjunctions and the Definite Article
supplemental_source_sections: 2014 intro: Pages 16-20 / Conjunctions, Prepositions and Other Grammatical Items; Definite Article; Prepositions
2014 appendix: Pages 46-55 / article rules, ו ההיפוך, and Appendix to 3.6G "The Word את"
2016 sample assessments: Page 88 / 3.3 Other vocabulary
review_items.reviewer_notes: Teacher review supports visible prefix and article identification as a foundational planning lane. The two functions of את should remain deferred until context rules and teacher-approved wording are added. This review does not authorize runtime activation or student-facing question generation.
unresolved_questions: Should the first review phase approve only visible prefix/article identification, while deferring interpretation-heavy cases such as the two functions of את?
How should the later 2025 split into 3.06 be reconciled with the older 2016 assessment material that still groups much of this content under 3.3 Other Vocabulary?
Which of the nikud-dependent distinctions, especially ו ההיפוך and article-prefix shifts, are source-clear enough to review now rather than later?
review_items.recommended_next_action: Revise 3.06 wording so visible prefix/article identification stays separate from the deferred functions of את, and keep the strand review-only while source and wording checks continue.

supplemental_source_sections: 2014 intro: Pages 35-43 / Verbs; Present, Future, and Past Tense; root changes
2014 appendix: Pages 22-27 and 50-53 / high-frequency verbs; ו ההיפוך rule material
2016 sample assessments: Pages 79-87 / 3.2 Vocabulary: שרשים (verbs)
review_items.reviewer_notes: Teacher review supports foundational tense, person, and form clues as a planning lane. Advanced areas such as binyan, passive forms, command forms, מקור, and שם הפועל should remain separate later lanes. This review does not authorize runtime activation or student-facing question generation.
unresolved_questions: Where should teacher review split foundational tense/person/form clues from later binyan, passive, command, and infinitive analysis?
Is the supplemental evidence strong enough for the full upper-level 2025 binyan/passive range, or only for foundational tense and root-sensitive recognition?
Should translation precision on verbs remain blocked until reviewers confirm exactly which form clues are considered introduced at each level?
review_items.recommended_next_action: Split foundational tense/person/form planning from later advanced verb lanes, then review the level mapping before any diagnostic blueprint use.

supplemental_source_sections: 2014 intro: Page 9 / Adjectives and Hebrew-to-English word order
2016 sample assessments: Pages 97-100 / 3.4 adjective matching and סמיכות examples
unresolved_questions: Is the partial supplemental support strong enough to treat 3.08 as a distinct reviewable diagnostic strand now, or should it stay deferred behind 3.04 phrase-structure review?
Should grouping and word-order work remain full-context only, rather than being prepared for any compact or isolated prompt format later?
Do the 2025 level descriptions map cleanly enough to later internal skill wording, especially where literal translation is provided but grouping is not?
review_items.recommended_next_action: Teacher-review Standard 3.08 directly against page 48 of the canonical PDF, confirm whether the partial older support is sufficient for a later diagnostic lane, and keep the strand fully review-only until that decision is made.

supplemental_source_sections: 2014 intro: Pages 47-53 / Vowels, sheva, dagesh, syllables, and related ניקוד rules
2014 appendix: Pages 46-51 / article ניקוד charts, prefix vocalization, and ו ההיפוך summaries
unresolved_questions: Are levels 1-4 intentionally outside the scope of 3.10, or does the extracted table need another manual pass to confirm the absence of lower-level expectations?
Which advanced ניקוד subskills, if any, should be reviewed in the same phase as prefix/article nikud distinctions from 3.06, and which should stay fully deferred?
How much trust should later reviewers place in OCR-sensitive nekudos and chart structure before consulting the raw PDF directly?
review_items.recommended_next_action: Teacher-review the advanced-only note and the levels 5-8 rows against the canonical PDF, confirm the intended level gate, and defer any runtime-adjacent use until the nekudos and charts are manually verified.
